Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". - simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

content.thinking:
This is an important fix! The user identified that ReelDevTool wasn't triggering the spin, and now they've added a mechanism to drive the reel animation during testing.

The key insight is: in normal gameplay, SpinState.OnProcess calls Spin(dt) every frame to advance the reel animation. But in test mode using ReelDevTool, the state machine stays in IDLE, so nothing is calling Spin(dt) - the reels would never animate.

The fix adds:
1. A flag `m_isDrivingSpin` to track when DevTool should drive the spin
2. Logic in update() to call `this.m_slotReel.Spin(dt)` every frame when the flag is true
3. Auto-stop when `IsStop` becomes true

This is a bugfix - the feature (ReelDevTool) was broken and is now fixed.

  <title>ReelDevTool now drives spin animation during testing</title>
  <subtitle>Added frame-by-frame spin driver to ReelDevTool since state machine remains idle during preview testing</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>ReelDevTool.update() now calls m_slotReel.Spin(dt) every frame when m_isDrivingSpin flag is true</fact>
    <fact>Normal gameplay flow has SpinState.OnProcess calling Spin(dt) but test mode stays in IDLE state with no driver</fact>
    <fact>Spin driver automatically stops when m_slotReel.IsStop becomes true and logs completion message</fact>
    <fact>Fix enables triggerStandardSpin preview functionality to actually animate reels in Unity editor</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>ReelDevTool's preview testing was failing because the reels wouldn't animate when triggerStandardSpin was enabled. Root cause: in normal gameplay, SpinState.OnProcess calls Spin(dt) every frame to advance the reel animation, but during ReelDevTool testing the state machine stays in IDLE state so no component is driving the spin forward. The fix adds a private m_isDrivingSpin flag and logic in update() to manually call this.m_slotReel.Spin(dt) every frame during test mode, stopping automatically when IsStop becomes true. This unblocks M0a acceptance testing which requires verifying reels spin and stop at the 5-column fake board configuration.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>

    <file>assets/Script/ReelDevTool.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <narrative>This completes the ReelDevTool spin animation fix. The previous edit added the frame-by-frame driving logic in update() that calls Spin(dt) when m_isDrivingSpin is true. This edit wires the trigger by setting m_isDrivingSpin = true in TestStandardSpin() after StartSpin() is called. Now when a developer checks triggerStandardSpin in the Unity Inspector, the reels will actually animate: StartSpin initializes the spin, m_isDrivingSpin gets set to true, and every frame update() advances the animation until IsStop becomes true. This unblocks M0a acceptance testing which requires verifying reels spin and stop correctly at the 5-column fake board.</narrative>
